meta.description: Personal health and training database — Todd Blackhurst AI coaching system

oura_biology_baselines.notes: Oura Biology personal baselines. VO2 Max 38.9 (Below Average — cardio fitness improvement goal). HRV baseline 32.5 ms (any reading below this signals recovery deficit). Avg sleep 6h 25m/night — chronic mild sleep debt.

nutrition_targets.notes: Targets set 2026-04-11. ~380 kcal/day deficit (between mild and moderate). Protein elevated for anabolic resistance at age 57. Fat raised for hormone support. Recalculate if weight changes ±3 kg or training frequency shifts.

body_composition.notes: Baseline from Bevel 30-day Progress Report (Nov 5 – Nov 24)
End-point from Bevel 30-day Progress Report — marked as Low on fat distribution
Bevel weekly scan (week of Apr 6). Visceral Fat Level 2.0. BF% 16.2%, weight 75.9 kg / 167.3 lbs. Primary VAT reduction target ongoing.

Hume Body Pod full scan, updated Apr 10. Air-displacement plethysmography (higher BF% than BIA/Bevel). Trunk muscle Low — core/anti-rotation priority confirmed. Bilateral arm fat High. Goal weight 165 lbs.

Bevel morning scan Apr 11, 4:29 AM. Weight stable at 166.0 lbs. Body fat 17.4% — excellent control. Lean mass 137.1 lbs (78.1% muscle) — preserved well. Visceral fat Lv 3.0 indicates modest VAT. BMR 1779 kcal baseline for deficit calculations.

Bevel morning scan Apr 12, 6:47 AM. Weight 165.8 lbs — down 0.2 lbs from Apr 11. BF 16.3% stable. Lean mass 138.8 lbs (79.1%). Visceral Fat Lv 2.0 (down from 3.0 on Apr 11 — positive signal). BMR 1779 kcal consistent. Trending toward 165 lb goal.

body_composition.body_fat_note: BIA underestimate — not reliable for body fat tracking; use Bevel and Hume ADP pod scan as primary reference
body_composition.notes: Hume Pod BIA daily scale Apr 12, 6:51 AM. Weight 166.8 lbs. Metabolic Age 42 yrs (Excellent). Health Score 673. Trunk muscle Low (consistent with ADP scan Apr 10). BF 10.2% is a BIA artifact — ADP scan (Apr 10) measured 22.0%. Segmental swings vs Apr 10 are hydration-driven, not real muscle change. Trunk Low rating structurally persistent.

Ocare3 BIA scan Apr 13, 06:11 AM. Weight 165.6 lbs — essentially AT goal weight (165 lbs). BF 15.7% (BIA — use Bevel/Hume ADP as primary reference). Muscle 79.6%, Visceral Fat Lv 2.0 stable. BMR 1778 kcal consistent. Last week avg 165.6, max 166.0, min 164.9 — trend holding at goal range.

Hume Pod daily Apr 13, 6:43 AM. Weight 166.8 lbs. Weekly avg 165.6 lbs — within 1 lb of 165 lb goal. Health Score 673 High, Biological Age 30 yrs (vs chronological 57). Steps 5,230 by 6:43 AM, Active Cal 261 (day ongoing). BF% from BIA not reliable — use ADP scan Apr 10 (22.0%) as reference.

recovery_sleep.notes: Low strain day — Oura. Productive Sleep Debt Recovery noted. Outdoor cycling session same morning.
Sleep Quality 55%, below average. Time in Bed 7h 43m, Asleep 7h 17m. Wind-down 9:20 PM, target bedtime 9:50 PM.
Recovery 58% Normal. Sleep 73% Above normal. Deep Sleep 59m (notable). HR Dip 14%. Sleep Bank -1h 49m. Cardio Load 29 Detraining. Sleep HRV avg 31ms / max 126ms. Lowest HR 56 bpm.
Significant HRV Drop — Recovery crashed to 26% (HRV 19.3 ms, ~20ms below baseline). Despite 1h 6min deep sleep. Sleep started Apr 3 at 10:19 PM. Reduction in training intensity indicated.
Recovery Breakthrough — HRV surged to 51.4 ms (+52% rebound from Apr 4's 26%). Nervous system adapting well to training. Sleep 5h 50m (started Apr 4 at 10:56 PM). Sleep Score 69 FAIR.

Recovery Slide 41% — HRV 36.2 ms (Recovery metric), 25 ms avg overnight. HRV/HR spike after Apr 8 high-strain session. Sleep Score 69 FAIR. 5h 28m asleep / 7h 4m in bed (9:21 PM – 4:25 AM). Efficiency 77%. Deep 38m, REM 65m. HR Dip 15%. Debt -2h 13m.

Resilience Signal — steady 57% recovery despite Apr 10 high-strain session. HR dropped 3.1 bpm. Rise: 6h 48m sleep / 8h 13m in bed (9:18 PM – 5:31 AM), Efficiency 83%, Deep 43m, REM 1h 18m, Latency 22m, Restfulness Good, Timing Optimal, Debt -2h 30m Moderate. AutoSleep: 7h 14m total, REM 2h 6m, Deep 30m, Debt -2h 8m, Sleep Time 9:38 PM. HR Dip 14%. Lowest HR 58. HRV avg 26ms / max 151ms. Avg HR 64. SpO2 95.2%.

Recovery Breakthrough — Oura 68% (Above normal, +12% from yesterday). Rise sleep score 70/GOOD. REM above normal at 2h 28m (Oura) / 1h 2m (Rise). HRV 41.7ms above personal baseline of 32.5ms. Sleep short at 6h 15m–6h 54m. Moderate sleep debt (~2h 12m). Latency 37m — attributed to elevated metabolism from prior workout. Restfulness flagged. Respiratory rate slightly below normal (14.1 rpm) — monitor. SpO2 96.1% normal. Training guidance: Amber-Green, full session OK, HR <122 bpm. | Motra supplement: HRV Stabilizing (7d +1.6ms, 30d -3.8ms), RHR 62.7 bpm Stable, VO2 Max 39.6 Good (+4.9 over 90d), Weight 166.3 lbs (30d high, +0.8 lbs/wk trend). Training status: Amber-Green confirmed.
recovery_sleep.motra_weight_trend: Increasing — 30-day new high; +0.8 lbs/wk trend; 30-day projection 169.3 lbs

recovery_sleep.recovery_trend: HRV drop after Recovery Breakthrough day — regression to mean
recovery_sleep.notes: HRV 20ms — significant drop from 41.7ms yesterday, well below ~32.5ms baseline. Sleep short at 5h19m (Oura)/5h34m (Rise). Sleep debt accumulating: -4h10m (Oura) / -3h23m (Rise). Positives: deep sleep 59min/18% solid, restfulness Optimal, HR stable. Fasted morning walk already done (5:16 AM, 2.43mi, Zone 0-1, 16% Activity Strain). Training guidance: Amber — proceed with programmed strength session, HR cap 122 bpm, treat first sets as warm-in, reduce Block C if fatigue surfaces.

meals.notes: Sugar gap. The peanut M&M's are the signature sweets pattern — 17g carbs, 8g fat, 3g protein. Swap: 1 oz beef jerky next time — same pocket snack, 10g protein, 0g fat, 0g carbs.

meals.notes: Best macro decision of the day — lean protein + rice carbs, minimal fat. Sashimi (6 slices) did most of the protein work at 20g. Roll added the carbs efficiently. Good instinct given the fat overrun earlier.

meals.notes: Retroactive addition — Cal AI screenshots uploaded Apr 13 morning. Late-night whey + almond milk at 8:45 PM. Closes the protein gap: updated Apr 12 protein = 141g (was 116g).

daily_totals.note: RETROACTIVELY UPDATED Apr 13 — late-night whey shake added. Final: 2104 kcal / 141g P / 135g C / 117g F. Protein gap closed to -9g (excellent). Calories 104 over target. Fat still over (+47g) due to breakfast walnuts + lunch ranch.

nutrition.notes: 5 entries logged via Cal AI. Fat target significantly blown (113g vs 70g target, +43g) — damage concentrated at breakfast (walnuts +26g) and lunch (ranch +39g). Dinner (sushi + sashimi) was a strong recovery choice: 31g protein, 54g carbs, only 8g fat. M&M snack at 4:15 PM was the sweets pattern showing up — 8g fat, 17g sugar. Day calories nearly on target at 1954/2000. Protein 34g short — one more whey shake would close it. Morning walk: 2.22 mi, 198 active cal, avg HR 101 bpm.

items.food: Gold Standard 100% Whey - Vanilla Ice Cream (×2 scoops)

meals.notes: Pre-strength-session shake. Strong protein start (55g). 41g carbs for fuel. Fat only 13g — excellent for a day with yesterday's 117g fat overrun. Two scoops whey is the right call on a training day.

items.food: Gold Standard 100% Whey - Vanilla Ice Cream (Optimum Nutrition)

meals.notes: Post-workout shake at 10:05 AM. Note: Cal AI shows 8g carbs + 12g fat — fat higher than typical due to 2 cups almond milk. Protein solid at 29g.
Lunch at 12:01 PM. Light on protein (6g only). Creamy potato soup — calorie-moderate but low protein impact. Fat budget still fine.

Lunch main course at 12:10 PM. BBQ ribs are the protein engine — 46g in one meal. Fat heavy at 38g (pork ribs + coleslaw dressing). Steamed veggies and Pocari Sweat round out the meal. Strong protein result.

daily_totals.note: Updated 12:10 — pre-workout + post-workout + potato soup + BBQ ribs/veggies/Pocari. Day ongoing. Remaining: ~454 kcal / 12g P / 97g C / fat target met (4g over).

activity_sessions.notes: Zone 3 dominant ride. HR well controlled for outdoor cycling.
High strain score (78%) despite moderate activity — short active window. Cardio load detraining zone.
Volume Spike flagged by Oura — nearly doubled 14-day avg for a single session. High chest and hinge day. HR stayed mostly in lower aerobic zones.

Volume Spike — nearly doubled 14-day average for single session. Warmup block included Cervical CARS, Thoracic Open Book, Glute Bridge, Band Pull Apart, Ext Shoulder Rotation, Kettlebell Swings. HR stayed mostly Z1-Z2. High chest and hinge focus.
Productive Shape Session — 7,764 kg volume. Cardio stayed within HRV limits. Added 13 pts to Cardio Load. Well-executed session.

activity_sessions.metabolic_opportunity: At 11 AM — good window to transition into planned session. Energy Bank at 71% despite 67% daily stress.
activity_sessions.notes: Oura snapshot at 11:00 AM. Recovery at 57% — moderate state. Low strain (6%) allows for structured session. Sleep quality 61% reflects solid rest. All vitals normal. Blood glucose 101 mg/dL stable via Stelo integration.

activity_sessions.pr_notes: Lifetime PR: Hip Abduction 54 reps in a single set. Total volume 15,409 lbs — 18% above 14-day average of 13,040 lbs. Cardio Status shifted from Maintaining to Peaking.
activity_sessions.notes: Glute Volume Spike session, Apr 6. Motra session ID 20260306 — naming discrepancy, actual date confirmed Apr 6, 2026 by timestamp and Monday day-of-week match. High-volume posterior chain day. All HR within Zone 1-2, well within <122 bpm constraint. Oura daily recovery 41%.

Morning walk in Beitun District. Avg HR 101 bpm — well within <122 bpm constraint. Easy effort, Zone 1. Good morning activation / low-intensity fat-burning cardio. Supports VAT reduction goal without adding training load.

Apple Fitness daily step summary as of 8:15 PM. 10,292 steps / 4.81 miles. Morning walk (2.22 mi) + general daily movement (2.59 mi). High NEAT day — well above average. Supports VAT reduction goal. No additional training load added.

Fasted morning walk, Beitun District. 71°F/86% humidity. Avg HR 96 bpm, Zone 0-1, 16% Activity Strain only. HR well within <122 bpm constraint. Low-intensity aerobic work — fasted state VAT reduction protocol. Pre-strength session burn.

Week 1 Monday — Pull + Glute Hinge. Added Pull-Ups (unplanned) and Wall Slides to PREP. 80 min session — OVER 65 min cap. Volume 6.3K kg / 475 kcal. Progressed Single-Arm Row from 10kg to 12kg in sets 3-4. Suitcase Carry heavy set dropped from 16kg to 12kg (fatigue management). Good execution of corrective block. HR context: Amber day (HRV 20ms, below 32.5ms baseline).

coaching_relevance.hip_offset: Right-side dominance confirmed — unilateral work and hip alignment cues critical
coaching_relevance.head_deviation: Forward head posture / cervical alignment deficit — cervical CARS and wall slides priority
coaching_relevance.spine_deviation: Significant lateral spine deviation (11.4 cm) — anti-rotation and quadratus lumborum work
coaching_relevance.right_arm_low_score: Right shoulder asymmetry — matches structural correction system priorities
coaching_relevance.flagged_muscles_summary: Posterior chain (traps, infraspinatus, lats, erectors) and anterior chain (serratus, oblique) imbalances both present

posture_assessment.notes: WorldGym Taiwan AI posture scan (Crosspoint system). Dynamic score 77/100. Hip offset -4.3 cm (right dominant). Spine deviation 11.4 cm is significant. Right arm 45 vs left arm 58 confirms shoulder asymmetry. Directly validates structural correction priorities in 20_STRUCTURAL_CORRECTION_PRIORITY_USER.md.

bullets: HRV 20ms — 12.5ms below your 32.5ms baseline. Sharp drop from yesterday's 41.7ms breakthrough — classic regression to mean after a high-HRV day, compounded by 3 nights of stacking sleep debt.

Sleep 5h19m (Oura) / 5h34m (Rise). Rise score 51 — Poor. Debt now -4h10m (Oura) / -3h23m (Rise). That's the steepest single-night debt reading in tracked history. The debt is accelerating, not stabilizing.
Positives: Deep sleep 59 min (best in recent history), restfulness Optimal, HR dip 15%, lowest HR 56 bpm. Sleep architecture was solid — duration alone is the problem.

Non-obvious pattern: Sleep debt over 3 consecutive nights: -2h30m → -2h12m → -4h10m. It's worsening each night. Wednesday's HRV reading is already in jeopardy before Tuesday begins. Tonight's bedtime before 10:00 PM is the only variable you control.

training_direction.call: SESSION COMPLETE ✅ — Pull + Glute Hinge done. Recovery mode for the rest of today.
bullets: Monday session logged: 07:56 AM, 80 min, 6,300 kg / 13,889 lbs, 475 kcal, 15 exercises. Progression: Single-Arm Row 10kg → 12kg in sets 3–4 ✅. Pull-ups added unplanned — good instinct, not a problem.

5 of 5 recent sessions exceeded the 65-min cap. Apr 1 = 95 min, Apr 6 = 96 min, Apr 8 = 118 min, Apr 10 = 77 min, Apr 13 = 80 min. Average overrun: 28 minutes. Wednesday cap enforcement starts before the foam roller — set the timer first.
Fasted walk done (2.43 mi, Zone 0-1, avg HR 96 bpm). No training for the rest of today. Recovery, nutrition, and sleep are the only active inputs between now and Wednesday 7am.
Next session: Wednesday Apr 15 — Push + Quad / Anti-Rotation. Floor 3 → Floor 2. Half-Kneeling Landmine Press, Incline DB Press, V-Squat, Pallof Press. 65-min hard cap — no exceptions.
Wednesday readiness hinges on tonight's sleep. If HRV comes in below 25ms Wednesday morning, Block B drops to 2 sets per exercise and Block C cuts to Pallof Press + Suitcase Carry only.

nutrition.binding_constraint: CARBS — 97g still available. Protein is essentially done (138g / 150g). Fat budget is closed (74g / 70g — 4g over).
bullets: Updated totals through 12:10 PM: 1,546 kcal / P: 138g / C: 93g / F: 74g. BBQ ribs (46g protein) at lunch flipped the protein picture — within 12g of daily target before 1 PM.
Fat is closed at 74g vs 70g target (4g over). No added fats at dinner tonight — lean protein only: fish, chicken breast, shrimp, egg whites, or low-fat cottage cheese.
Carbs: 97g remaining (190g target). Direct all of them to dinner — rice, sweet potato, or both. Carbs at dinner support glycogen and sleep-quality. Don't leave them on the table.
Remaining protein: 12g only. One serving of fish or chicken closes it. No extra shake needed unless dinner protein falls short.
Log dinner tonight — a photo is enough. Keep the 3-entry streak going.

progress.top_number: 165.6 lbs weekly avg — you are at goal weight. Phase shifts to body composition now.
bullets: Weight: Ocare3 this morning 165.6 lbs / Hume Pod 166.8 lbs (hydration variance). Weekly avg 165.6 lbs, weekly min 164.9 lbs. The 165 lb target is achieved — weight management phase begins.

Body comp (Bevel Apr 12): BF 16.3%, lean mass 138.8 lbs (79.1%), Visceral Fat Level 2.0 — down from 3.0 the day before. Lean mass is trending up while in caloric deficit at 57. That's the core result.
VO2 Max: 39.6 ml/kg/min (Motra Apr 12) — +4.9 over 90 days, now above Oura baseline of 38.9. First consistent upward trend in tracked history.
Key win this week: Today's pull session showed strength progression — Single-Arm Row from 10kg → 12kg while on an Amber readiness day. Muscle is being preserved under deficit.

Key risk: Motra 30-day weight trend projects 169.3 lbs by mid-May at +0.8 lbs/wk. That contradicts Bevel and Ocare3 data. If weekly avg climbs above 167 lbs by Apr 20, tighten deficit and re-evaluate targets.

watch_for: If tonight's bedtime is after 10:00 PM or sleep is again under 6 hours — flag Wednesday's brief immediately at 6 AM and cap the session at 55 min with Block B reduced to 2 sets per exercise. Three consecutive debt nights make a third straight Amber session almost certain.

If afternoon snack today includes chocolate, M&M's, or sweets — swap now to 1 oz beef jerky or a whey shake. Yesterday at 4:15 PM the M&M pattern hit — 140 kcal, 17g sugar, 8g fat for 3g protein. The swap nets +7g protein and saves the fat budget.

If Wednesday morning HRV comes in below 25ms — reduce Block B to 2 sets per exercise and cut Block C to Pallof Press + Suitcase Carry only (no third element). Do not compensate with extra reps — that's exactly how Monday stretched to 80 minutes.

session_plan.session_goal: Build lat width and horizontal pull strength while training glute-dominant hinge patterns and structural correction — completed this morning.
